summary: Frontend-only e2e testing of THE DENIM ARCHIVE single-page site. All requested data-testid flows verified: hero counter reaches 42,817; pixel cursor present; archive filters/sort/detail modal/flip/download PNG; film modal opens with controls and unmuted; upload dropzone loading -> result -> reset; social reel muted by default then unmuted on click; all 9 sections + footer render; no console/page runtime errors (only a benign framer-motion scroll offset warning).

issues: Framer-motion warns: 'Please ensure that the container has a non-static position...' — likely from a useScroll target without position:relative/fixed/absolute. Non-blocking but should be silenced by adding position class to the scroll target container in Archive Tile or Hero.

action_items: Optional: fix framer-motion scroll-offset warning by ensuring useScroll targets have non-static position (e.g., add className 'relative' to the Tile motion.button in Archive.jsx). Purely a warning; no functional impact.
critical_code_review_comments: hero-film.mp4 is intentionally absent (placeholder); videos will not load but elements/controls are configured correctly as intended per PRD.
Upload accepts JPG/PNG up to 10MB and validates mime type — OK.
downloadEvidenceCard triggers a real PNG download (verified filename denim-archive-A-XXXXX.png).
All key interactive elements expose data-testid — good for testability.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend not used. Verified flows: hero counter, custom pixel cursor (img.pixel-cursor), archive filters (stains/tears/fades/repairs/all-evidence), sort (latest/oldest), archive tile -> detail modal -> ArchiveCard flip and PNG download, film modal (controls=true, muted=false), upload JPG/PNG -> loading -> result -> reset, social-reel (muted default -> unmuted on click). All 9 sections + footer render without runtime errors. Only a benign framer-motion useScroll positioning warning appears in console.
